description: Not moving or making a sound; calm and tranquil. 'Moesha' names the quality of stillness—the absence of movement, the presence of calm.
sound_symbolism: The word rests completely. 'Mo' grounds with settled presence, movement ceased. The vowel 'e' opens to spacious calm. Then 'shao' /ʃa/ breathes softly, stillness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'moesha mira' (still water). Related to 'maeli' (quiet) but emphasizes absence of motion rather than absence of sound.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Stillness enables insight—the unmoving mind perceives what agitation obscures.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Still presence supports others—not rushing to fill silence, allowing space for what needs to emerge.
